questions.question: Define a discrete random variable and give two examples relevant to computer science.
keyPoints: Discrete RV takes countable distinct values
Example 1: Number of packet losses in network transmission
Example 2: Number of failed login attempts
Example 3: Number of customers served in queue

questions.question: State and prove the linearity of expectation property for discrete random variables.
keyPoints: Proof uses definition: Σ(ax + b)p(x) = aΣxp(x) + bΣp(x)
Since Σp(x) = 1, we get aE[X] + b

questions.question: A software company receives on average 3 bug reports per day. Assuming a Poisson distribution, find the probability of receiving exactly 5 bug reports tomorrow.
Explain the difference between Binomial and Poisson distributions with appropriate examples.
keyPoints: Binomial: fixed n trials, two outcomes, p constant
Poisson: counts rare events in interval, unlimited potential trials
Binomial example: number of heads in 10 coin tosses
Poisson example: number of emails received in an hour

questions.question: If X and Y are independent random variables with E[X]=4, E[Y]=6, Var(X)=2, Var(Y)=3, find E[X+Y] and Var(X+Y).
keyPoints: Using formula Var(X+Y) = Var(X) + Var(Y) + 2Cov, and Cov=0 for independent

questions.question: Define the cumulative distribution function (CDF) of a discrete random variable and state its three properties.
keyPoints: F(x) = P(X ≤ x) = Σ p(t) for t ≤ x
Non-decreasing: if x1 < x2 then F(x1) ≤ F(x2)
lim x→-∞ F(x) = 0, lim x→∞ F(x) = 1

questions.question: A server handles requests independently with probability of failure 0.1. If it processes 20 requests, find probability of at most 2 failures using binomial distribution.
Derive the mean and variance of the Binomial distribution B(n, p). Show all steps.
keyPoints: Define X ~ Bin(n,p) as sum of n independent Bernoulli(p) variables
E[X] = E[X₁ + X₂ + ... + Xₙ] = n × E[Xᵢ] = n × p
For variance: Var(X) = Var(ΣXᵢ) = ΣVar(Xᵢ) since independent
Final answer: Mean = np, Variance = np(1-p)

questions.question: State the conditions for a function to be a valid probability mass function. Also, verify if the following is a valid PMF: P(X=k) = k/15 for k = 1,2,3,4,5.
keyPoints: Conditions: p(x) ≥ 0 for all x, Σ p(x) = 1

questions.question: Explain the memoryless property of the Geometric distribution. A user tries to login with probability of success 0.2 each attempt. Find probability that more than 3 attempts are needed.
keyPoints: Memoryless: P(X > m+n | X > m) = P(X > n)
Meaning: past failures don't affect future probability

questions.question: Two discrete random variables X and Y have joint PMF: P(X=i, Y=j) = k(i+j) for i=1,2 and j=1,2. Find: (a) the value of k, (b) marginal PMFs, (c) E[X] and E[Y], (d) Cov(X,Y).
